isolation.scope: Used one fresh isolated browser session against http://localhost:3001 and only mutated that session's in-browser game state.

assertions.title: Engine Responds to User Move
assertions.reason: After White played a legal move on Easy, Claudefish replied with a legal move well within 10 seconds and the reply appeared on both the board and the move history.

steps.action: Start a fresh Easy game and play e2-e4
steps.expected: Engine replies with a legal Black move within 10 seconds
steps.observed: Timed Easy run replied with 1...e5; screenshot rerun replied with 1...c6. Both replies were legal and rendered on the board.

steps.action: Check the history panel after the reply
steps.expected: The move list shows both half-moves
steps.observed: History updated to 2 ply / 1 full move with White's e4 and the engine's reply.

evidence.network: Preflight curl GET / -> 200 in 0.030s; no per-move network traffic was required because search ran in the local WASM worker.
observations: Timed Easy run final history was 1. e4 e5.
Screenshot rerun final history was 1. e4 c6.
Both Easy replies completed far below the 10-second contract.

assertions.title: Difficulty Easy vs Maximum
assertions.reason: Easy replies were effectively immediate, while switching to Maximum mid-game changed the very next engine move to a long visible search (~5.4s) and later Maximum also converted a blunder line with Qh4#, demonstrating stronger tactical play.

steps.action: Play an Easy move from a fresh game
steps.observed: The Easy timing run completed within an upper bound of 0.408s end-to-end from move initiation.

steps.action: In the same browser session, switch from Easy to Maximum mid-game before White's next move
steps.expected: The next engine reply uses Maximum settings
steps.observed: From the position 1. e4 e6, switching to Maximum before 2. a3 produced a visible 'Maximum · Depth 12 · 5s' search and the next reply 2...Nc6 arrived after ~5.426s.

steps.action: Continue a Maximum game through a tactical blunder line
steps.expected: Maximum should play stronger tactical moves than Easy
steps.observed: In the line 1. f3 d5 2. g4 e5 3. h3, Maximum found 3...Qh4# and ended the game.

observations: Easy run produced 1...e5 in the timed run and 1...c6 in the screenshot rerun.
Maximum mid-game switch took effect on the next engine move only, not retroactively.
Maximum later found the mating move Qh4# in the tested blunder line.

assertions.title: Evaluation Bar
assertions.reason: The evaluation bar started near equality and changed after moves, eventually showing a mate score in the checkmate position.

steps.action: Observe the evaluation after engine-played positions
steps.observed: The value changed to +0.3 after the Easy e4/e6 line, to -0.0 after the Maximum 1. e4 e6 2. a3 Nc6 line, and to -M1 after the checkmate.

observations: Start: Evaluation bar +0.1.
After Easy e4/e6: Evaluation bar +0.3.
After Maximum 1. e4 e6 2. a3 Nc6: Evaluation bar -0.0.
After checkmate: Evaluation bar -M1.

assertions.title: Thinking Indicator
assertions.reason: During the Maximum search from 1. e4 e6 2. a3, the thinking badge was visibly rendered with the selected level and disappeared once 2...Nc6 was played.

steps.action: Switch to Maximum and play 2. a3 in the live game
steps.expected: A visible thinking indicator appears while the engine searches
steps.observed: The badge displayed 'Claudefish is thinking…' with 'Maximum · Depth 12 · 5s'.

steps.expected: The badge disappears when the move is shown
steps.observed: The badge cleared when the board and history updated with 2...Nc6.

observations: Easy replies were too brief for a stable badge screenshot, so the visible-thinking proof used the deeper Maximum off-book move 2. a3.
The indicator disappeared cleanly when the engine move rendered.

assertions.title: Game End Detection and Display
assertions.reason: A complete game reached checkmate with a visible result banner, the winner text was correct, a New Game option was present, and post-game board interaction was blocked.

steps.action: Play a full game to checkmate
steps.expected: Checkmate shows a game-over message and winner
steps.observed: The line 1. f3 d5 2. g4 e5 3. h3 Qh4# produced the overlay 'Checkmate — Claudefish wins.'

steps.action: Try to interact with the board after the game ends
steps.observed: A post-game click on 'white knight on b1' was blocked because the game-over overlay intercepted pointer events; history stayed at 6 ply and the knight remained on b1.
The game-over overlay displayed its own New Game button.

observations: Overlay text: 'GAME OVER' / 'Checkmate — Claudefish wins.' / 'Your king has been mated.'
Status area matched the overlay and showed last move Qh4#.
Post-game state remained at History: 6 ply; white knight stayed on b1 and did not appear on c3.

assertions.title: Web Worker Non-Blocking
assertions.reason: During a visible Maximum-depth search, requestAnimationFrame kept firing well under the 100ms threshold, so the main thread remained responsive while the worker searched.

steps.action: Switch to Maximum and play an off-book move (2. a3) so the engine performs a real search
steps.expected: Depth >= 6 search runs while the UI remains responsive
steps.observed: The badge showed 'Maximum · Depth 12 · 5s' and the reply 2...Nc6 arrived after ~5.426s.

steps.action: Measure requestAnimationFrame timing during the visible search
steps.observed: Recorded frame gaps stayed mostly in the ~11-24ms range with a worst observed gap of 47.5ms.

observations: Representative frame gaps from the monitored search: 6.0ms, 8.3ms, 17.2ms, 23.6ms, 26.4ms, 47.5ms.
No hang or frozen UI was observed during the measured search.

assertions.title: Complete Game to Checkmate
assertions.reason: A full game from the initial position reached checkmate and every move in the tested sequence rendered on the board and in history before the final game-over overlay appeared.

steps.action: Start from the initial position and play a full game against the engine
steps.expected: Moves keep rendering correctly through checkmate
steps.observed: The tested line was 1. f3 d5 2. g4 e5 3. h3 Qh4#.

steps.action: Inspect the board, history, and end state
steps.expected: History matches the played game and the final position is checkmate
steps.observed: The board showed Black's queen on h4, the move history ended with Qh4#, and the game-over overlay declared Claudefish the winner.

observations: Start screenshot captured the untouched initial position.
Mid-game screenshot captured the live position after 1. f3 d5 2. g4 e5.
The final screenshot captured the checkmate overlay and locked board.

frictions.description: Easy replies were so fast that the thinking badge could not be captured reliably on Easy.
frictions.resolution: Used an off-book Maximum move (2. a3 after 1. e4 e6) to capture a stable visible-search state and collect non-blocking timing evidence.

frictions.description: After checkmate, both the page controls and the overlay exposed a 'New Game' button, so strict role+name lookup was ambiguous.
frictions.resolution: Used the overlay button ref from the annotated screenshot when a deterministic post-checkmate reset was needed.

summary: Tested 7 assigned engine-play website assertions; all 7 passed. Easy replies completed in well under 2 seconds, switching to Maximum mid-game made the next reply take ~5.4 seconds with the thinking badge visible, requestAnimationFrame stayed under 100ms during the worker search, and a full game ended in the live checkmate line 1. f3 d5 2. g4 e5 3. h3 Qh4# with the board locked afterward.
